sections.paragraph:
διηνεκῶς adv. (s. prec. entry; Aeschyl., Ag. 319 [Hom., Hes. διηνεκέως; Mayser 13, n. 4]; OGI 194, 12; 544, 19; PGM 4, 1219; LXX; Philo, Sacr. Abel. 94) adv. fr. διηνεκής continually, without lapse 1 Cl 24:1.—M-M. s.v. διηνεκής.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

prec. prec. = preceding

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 245
